text: We are presenting robotic-assisted right adrenalectomy with IVC thrombectomy.
Patient, a fifty-eight-year-old male with diabetes mellitus, incidentally detected of right adrenal mass with IVC thrombus, Hb of twelve and creat of point ninety-three.
MRI suggested of six into four point four into six point four centimeter right adrenal mass with level two IVC thrombus with query IVC volume region, probability of adrenocortical carcinoma.
This is the MRI coronal section showing the right adenine tumor and the IVC thrombus. This is the MRI axial section showing the same.
Da Vinci SI system was used. First integrated liver care team operated.
The patient is positioned supine as the surgeon mobilizes the hepatic lobe. The supra-hepatic inferior vena cava (IVC) is looped, followed by looping around the porta hepatis.
The patient is in a supine position with visible port placements for the Da Vinci SI system. The falciform ligament is divided, and the liver is dissected free from all anterior attachments.
The surgeon mobilizes the left lobe of the liver, followed by the division of the gastro-hepatic ligament. Subsequently, the right lobe of the liver is mobilized.
The surgeon begins paracaval dissection, carefully dissecting tissue anterior to the suprahepatic portion of the inferior vena cava (IVC). The dissection continues along the paracaval region.
The surgeon clips and cuts multiple short hepatic veins using the Da Vinci SI robotic system, visible within the surgical field.

An infant feeding tube is passed through the suprahepatic portion of the inferior vena cava (IVC) from the right side of the liver and is retrieved from the left side. The suprahepatic IVC is looped and controlled.
The surgeon loops the protohepatic structure using a cut Follis catheter, securing it in place.
The surgeon dissects tissue anterior to the infrahepatic portion of the inferior vena cava (IVC), exposing the anterior wall of the IVC.

The patient is positioned on the right flank, and right adrenectomy ports are placed. The urology team performs a right adrenectomy with IVC thrombectomy, with visible port placement and docking in the surgical field.
The surgeon dissects the ascending colon, carrying the dissection medially and laterally around the inferior vena cava (IVC) in the suprarenal portion.

The surgeon loops the suprarenal portion of the inferior vena cava (IVC) and carries out dissection between the right adrenal tumor and the upper pole of the kidney. Dissection is also performed between the lateral wall of the IVC and the adrenal tumor.
The surgeon dissects the adrenal gland and tumor, carefully separating them from all surrounding attachments, revealing the anatomical structures involved.
The surgeon uses intraoperative ultrasound to locate the precise site of the IVC thrombus, followed by clipping and cutting the hepatic veins as part of the procedure.
The surgeon loops the infrahepatic portion of the inferior vena cava (IVC) above the IVC thrombus, securing the vessel in preparation for further surgical steps.
The surgeon tightens the loops and applies clamps before making an incision at the junction of the right adrenal vein and the inferior vena cava (IVC).
The surgeon performs an IVC thrombectomy, removing the thrombus and placing it alongside the excised right adrenal tumor.
The surgeon flushes the inferior vena cava (IVC) and closes the cavotomy using a 5-0 Gotic suture, ensuring the vessel is securely sealed.

The surgeon flushes the upper and lower separate sutures with heparin and places a knot in the middle. The IVC clamps are released, and there is no visible bleeding from the IVC. The specimen is placed in a bag.
The surgeon releases the control on the porta hepatis and supra-hepatic IVC, achieving complete hemostasis with no visible bleeding.
IVC clamp time of eleven minutes, console time of two hundred and one minute, operative time of three hundred and thirteen minutes, blood loss of seventy ml.
This is the specimen and the cut section. There are no postoperative complications. Patient discharged on the postoperative day for HPR suggestive adenocortical carcinoma, IVC thrombus free of tumor.
